findings.summary: GPU solver code (gpu_beam_search.py, gpu_sequential_solver.py, live_gpu_stream.py) has zero unit tests
findings.evidence: The three most complex files (2266+1450+2388 = 6100 lines) have no unit tests. CPU-testable functions like cpu_verify(), pre_simulate_locked(), SolveConfig/SolveCallbacks construction remain untested.
findings.suggestion: Add CPU-only tests for cpu_verify(), cpu_verify_detailed(), pre_simulate_locked(), SolveConfig/SolveCallbacks construction. Mock torch.cuda for GPU-dependent paths.

findings.summary: At least 15 Python files are stale solver generations without DEPRECATED markers
findings.evidence: Multiple solver generations from CPU beam search to Numba CUDA exist without deprecation markers. Only planner_v2.py and planner_v3.py have DEPRECATED notices. These files form stale dependency clusters.
findings.suggestion: Add DEPRECATED module-level docstrings to all stale solver files (beam_search.py, optimizer.py, seq_solver.py, gpu_optimizer.py, gpu_sim.py, smart_policy.py, order_solver.py, trip_solver.py, pipeline_solver.py, two_phase_planner.py, solve.py, solve_and_save.py, pipeline.py).

findings.summary: refine_from_solution uses raw kwargs while solve_sequential migrated to SolveConfig/SolveCallbacks
findings.evidence: solve_sequential() accepts SolveConfig and SolveCallbacks dataclass objects with legacy kwargs bridge. refine_from_solution() still uses 15 individual keyword arguments with no config object support.
findings.suggestion: Add SolveConfig/SolveCallbacks support to refine_from_solution() with the same legacy kwargs bridge as solve_sequential().

findings.summary: Difficulty detection logic duplicated in JS endpoints and Python configs.py
findings.evidence: iterate/+server.js detectDifficultyFromLog() hardcodes bot-count-to-difficulty mapping (1=easy, 3=medium, 5=hard, 10=expert) that duplicates Python configs.py detect_difficulty().
findings.suggestion: Extract difficulty detection into a shared JS constant or have the Python scripts emit difficulty in their JSON output, eliminating the JS-side reimplementation.
